common.loadingTimeout: This is taking longer than expected. Please wait or reload the page.
common.loadingHint: This may take a few seconds...
common.retryHint: If the problem persists: make sure your OpenCloudTouch server is reachable and reload the page.

player.playbackFailed: Playback control failed. Please try again.

presets.title: Presets
presets.playFailed: Preset could not be played. Please try again.
presets.infoItem1: Click an empty preset to assign a station
presets.infoItem2: Click an assigned station to change or delete it
presets.infoItem5: ⚙️ opens the Setup Wizard for the device
presets.infoItem6: "Load from device" syncs presets from SoundTouch
presets.cloudDependentDesc: This preset may require Bose cloud services
presets.cloudDependentNote: May no longer be available after May 6, 2026. Consider reconfiguring with direct streams.
presets.searchFailed: Station search failed. Please try again.
presets.confirmOverwriteMessage: Do you really want to overwrite preset {{preset}}?
presets.confirmDeleteMessage: Do you really want to delete preset {{preset}}?

manualIps.description: Add IP addresses of devices manually if automatic detection does not work.
manualIps.infoHint: Click "Discover Devices" to start detection manually. Found devices will appear on the home screen.

logging.description: Control the log detail level and download logs for troubleshooting.
logging.downloadDescription: Download the last 1000 backend log entries as a text file.

manualIpModal.title: Manual IP Configuration
manualIpModal.description: Enter the IP addresses of your devices (one per line or comma-separated).
manualIpModal.hint: You can find the IP address in the associated app under Settings → Info or in your router.

multiroom.title: Zones
multiroom.noActiveZonesHint: Select at least 2 devices to create a zone.
multiroom.infoItem1: The first selected device automatically becomes the master
multiroom.infoItem2: Click ★ to set another device as master
multiroom.infoItem3: Master and slaves play the same music in sync
multiroom.infoItem4: Volume can be adjusted individually per device
multiroom.infoItem5: Click the zone name to rename it

firmware.title: Firmware Updates
firmware.description: Manage firmware updates for your SoundTouch devices.
firmware.warningText: Firmware updates are experimental and may damage your device. Only use official firmware files from your device manufacturer. Upload is currently disabled.
firmware.uploadNotAvailable: Firmware upload is currently not available
firmware.uploadHint: This feature will be enabled in future versions
firmware.infoItem1: Firmware updates should only be performed if there are problems
firmware.infoItem2: Do not power off the device during an update
firmware.infoItem3: The update process can take 5–10 minutes
firmware.infoItem4: The device restarts automatically after the update

setup.title: Setup Wizard
setup.noDevicesHint: Please synchronize your SoundTouch devices first.
setup.noDevicesHint2: Go to the home page and click "Search for devices now".

step1.label: USB
step1.description: Prepare USB drive as configuration medium

step2.label: Restart
step2.description: Restart device and establish SSH access

step4.label: Connection
step4.description: Redirect configuration file to OCT server

step5.label: DNS
step5.description: Set up network redirect for Bose domains

step6.label: Verification
step6.description: Test configuration and verify DNS redirect

step2.title: Prepare USB drive
step2.description: Format a USB drive and create the configuration file.
step2.warning: The USB drive will be completely erased! Back up all data first.
step2.nextDisabled: Please check all steps above to continue.
step2.adapterNeeded: Your device has a Micro-USB port. You need an OTG adapter:
step2.windowsStep2: Right-click the USB drive in Explorer → Format
step2.macStep3: Select USB drive on the left
step2.createFileDesc: Create a file named <0>remote_services</0> (without extension) in the root directory of the USB drive. The file must be <1>empty</1> (no content).
step2.checkFat32: USB drive is formatted with FAT32
step2.checkFileEmpty: File 'remote_services' is in the root directory and empty
step2.checkReady: USB drive is ready to insert into the device

step3.title: Restart device
step3.description: Insert the USB drive and restart the device.
step3.warning: Do NOT remove the USB drive during restart!
step3.instructionStep1Desc: Insert the prepared USB drive into the device.
step3.instructionStep4Desc: Plug the power adapter back in.
step3.instructionStep5Desc: The device restarts and reads the remote_services file from the USB drive.
step3.statusAvailableHint: The device is ready for configuration.
step3.sshIntro: SSH access was temporarily enabled via the remote_services file. Please answer 3 questions to assess whether SSH should remain permanently on the device.
step3.q1: Is your home network protected by a router or firewall from unauthorized internet access?
step3.q2: Do only known, trusted people have access to your Wi-Fi network?
step3.q3: Are you planning to install firmware updates for this SoundTouch device in the near future?
step3.riskHintLow: SSH can be permanently enabled — your network is well protected.
step3.riskHintMedium: Weigh the options: permanent SSH slightly increases the attack surface.
step3.riskHintHigh: Permanent SSH is not recommended. Unknown factors or open networks significantly increase the risk.
step3.advancedTitle: For advanced users: test SSH connection manually
step3.advancedWhyText: Only if the automatic test above fails and you want to verify the connection yourself. For the normal process, you can skip this step.
step3.advancedDesc: SoundTouch devices require legacy SSH algorithms.
step3.puttyStep1: Session: Host Name = {{ip}}, Port = 22, Connection type = SSH
step3.puttyStep2: Connection → SSH → Kex: place Diffie-Hellman group 1 at the top
step3.puttyStep3: Connection → SSH → Cipher: place 3DES or AES at top; ensure aes128-cbc is not disabled
step3.puttyStep4: Connection → SSH → Auth: enable 'Allow attempted changes of username'; under Host keys remove rsa-sha2-256 and rsa-sha2-512, keep only ssh-rsa
step3.puttyStep5: Login: Username = root, Password = empty (press Enter)
step3.troubleshootingItem1: Check that the USB drive is correctly inserted
step3.troubleshootingItem2: Make sure the 'remote_services' file is correct
step3.troubleshootingItem3: Wait at least 60 seconds after restart
step3.troubleshootingItem5: Check that the device is on the same network
step3.troubleshootingItem6: Test the SSH connection manually with the command above — if that works, the device is ready.

step4.title: Create backup
step4.description: Create a full backup of the device.
step4.warning: A backup is REQUIRED! Without a backup you CANNOT restore the device if problems occur.
step4.backupInfo: All partitions will be backed up: RootFS (~58 MB), Persistent (~10 KB) and Update (~1 MB).
step4.progressNote: This can take up to 2 minutes...
step4.successHint: The backups were written to your USB drive. Remove the drive after the wizard — it contains your recovery files.

step5.descProxy: An HTTPS reverse proxy was detected on port 443. The BMX URL does not need to be changed.
step5.warningNoProxy: This change redirects radio requests to your OpenCloudTouch server.
step5.nextDisabledApply: Please apply the configuration successfully first.
step5.reasonValue: The reverse proxy on port 443 intercepts HTTPS requests and forwards them to OpenCloudTouch.
step5.proxyHint: Click 'Next' to configure the /etc/hosts file in the next step.
step5.strategyMessageProxy: HTTPS reverse proxy detected on port 443. Only the /etc/hosts file needs to be changed.
step5.strategyMessageBmx: No reverse proxy detected on port 443. The BMX URL must also be changed.
step5.configDesc: Enter the URL of your OpenCloudTouch server. This replaces the Bose bmxRegistryUrl.
step5.urlHint:
Examples: http://192.168.1.100:7777, oct.local, 192.168.1.100:8080
Default port: 7777, default protocol: http
step5.validationEmpty: Server URL must not be empty
step5.validationInvalid:
Invalid format. Expected: (http://)hostname(:port) or (http://)IP(:port).
Examples:
  • http://192.168.1.100:7777
  • oct.local
  • 192.168.1.100:8080
  • http://myserver:7777

step6.title: Modify hosts file
step6.description: Redirect Bose domains to your OpenCloudTouch server.
step6.warning: After this change, a device restart is required for DNS changes to take effect.
step6.nextDisabled: Please apply the hosts file successfully first.
step6.ipHint: IP address of your OpenCloudTouch server (IPv4 or IPv6)
step6.domainRequiredTitle: This domain is required for internet radio to continue working. It cannot be deselected.
step6.domainRequiredAria: Required domain: needed for internet radio

step7.title: Verification & Test
step7.description: Finalize device configuration, reboot, and verify everything works.
step7.warning: We recommend that all checks pass before you continue.
step7.rebootHint: The /etc/hosts changes only take effect after a restart. Restart the device before running the tests.
step7.rebootMandatoryHint: A reboot is required for the configuration changes to take effect. The device must restart before verification.
step7.rebootActionHint: Only if tests fail and you are sure a restart is needed
step7.rebootDone: Restart complete — device should be reachable again.
step7.checkConfigIdentical: All config files have identical content
step7.checkSourcesMissing: Missing sources: {{missing}}. Preset playback may fail for these source types.
step7.checkHostsDomainsMissing: Some required domains are missing from /etc/hosts
step7.checkHostsIpWrong: Not all host entries point to the expected IP
step7.checkSysConfigUuidMismatch: UUID in SystemConfigurationDB.xml does not match device
step7.fullVerifyItem1: Account UUID visible in device info
step7.fullVerifyItem2: All music sources registered (AUX, TuneIn, Bluetooth, ...)
step7.fullVerifyItem4: DNS redirects resolve to OpenCloudTouch server

uuid_present.fail: Device has no account UUID. Boot sync will not work.

uuid_in_db.fail: UUID not registered in OCT database. Streaming endpoint cannot resolve this device.

sources_complete.fail: Missing sources: {{missing}}. Preset playback may fail for these source types.

config_files_present.fail: Missing config file: {{missing}}. Firmware may not find OCT redirect on reboot.

config_files_identical.pass: All config files have identical content
config_files_identical.fail: Config files differ. Device may use wrong BMX URL after reboot.

config_bmx_url.fail: BMX URL still points to Bose cloud ({{bmx_url}}). Device will not contact OCT.

hosts_oct_block.fail: No OCT block in /etc/hosts. Domain redirects are missing.

hosts_domains_complete.fail: Missing host entries: {{missing}}. Some Bose services will not be redirected.

hosts_ip_correct.pass: All host entries point to correct IP
hosts_ip_correct.fail: Host entries point to wrong IP. Check for stale entries.

system_config_present.fail: SystemConfigurationDB.xml missing. Device may not initialize properly.

system_config_uuid_match.fail: UUID in SystemConfigurationDB.xml does not match device UUID

step7.allPassedMessage: Your device is fully configured and ready for use. All system checks and DNS redirects are working correctly.
step7.someFailedMessage: Not all verification checks passed. Review the results above and retry.
step7.failedStep1: Restart the device (disconnect and reconnect power)
step7.btnSkip: Continue anyway — device will not be marked as configured

step8.title: Setup complete!
step8.description: Your device has been successfully configured and is ready to use.
step8.heroMessage: {{device}} has been successfully configured for OpenCloudTouch.
step8.backupNote: Keep this backup safe! You can use it to restore your device in an emergency.
step8.noBackupWarning: No backup of your device was created. Please back up your device manually before making further changes.
step8.nextStep1Desc: You can now remove the USB drive from the device.
step8.nextStep2Desc: Your device can now access internet radio and presets via OpenCloudTouch.
step8.nextStep3Desc: Repeat the wizard for additional SoundTouch devices.

choice.title: What would you like to do?
choice.restore_desc: Undo all OCT changes and restore factory state

restore_choice.title: Choose Restore Type
restore_choice.description: Select how you want to restore the device.
restore_choice.clean_desc: Remove all OCT modifications. No backup needed.
restore_choice.backup_desc: Restore original config files from USB backup.

backup_scan.title: Scanning for Backups
backup_scan.description: Searching USB stick for backup files...
backup_scan.no_usb: No USB stick detected. Please insert USB stick and retry.

verification.title: Waiting for Device
verification.description: Device is rebooting. Waiting for it to come back online...
verification.timeout: Device not detected after 120s. It may have received a new IP via DHCP.
verification.manual_confirm: Device is back — I can see it

completion.title: Restore Complete
completion.note: The device is now in its original state. You can safely disconnect it.

errors.connectionFailed: Could not connect to the device.
errors.discoveryFailed: Discovery failed. Check your network settings.
errors.loadFailed: Failed to load. Please try again.
errors.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
errors.networkFailed: Could not connect to the server. Please check your network connection.
errors.timeout: The request took too long. Please try again.
errors.connectionRefused: The server is not reachable. Please restart the server.
errors.badRequest: Invalid request. Please check your inputs.
errors.notFound: The requested resource was not found.
errors.conflict: Conflict — the action could not be performed.
errors.serverError: Server error. Please try again later.
errors.presetsLoadFailed: Presets could not be loaded. Please try again.
errors.presetsSyncFailed: Presets could not be synchronized. Please try again.
errors.presetSaveFailed: Preset could not be saved. Please try again.
errors.presetClearFailed: Preset could not be deleted. Please try again.
errors.presetPlayFailed: Preset could not be played. Please try again.
errors.noDevices: No devices found. Please check if the devices are switched on.
errors.sshFailed: SSH connection failed. Please check the connection settings.
errors.portCheckFailed: Port check failed. Please check the network configuration.
errors.hostsFailed: Hosts file could not be changed.
errors.accessFailed: Access failed. Please check the permissions.
errors.devicesLoadMessage: Devices could not be loaded. Please check the connection and try again.
errors.pageNotFoundDescription: The requested page does not exist or has been moved.
errors.errorBoundaryMessage: An unexpected error occurred. Please reload the page.
errors.offlineDetail: "{{name}}" is offline or not on the network.
errors.offlineDetailNoName: The device is offline or not on the network.

about.appDescription: Local control for Bose SoundTouch devices

about.creditsDescription: Special thanks to the community members who contributed research, testing, and reverse engineering.

discovery.failed: Error during device search. Please try again.

discovery.step1Desc: Make sure your devices are powered on and connected to the same network.
discovery.step2Desc: Click "Search now" to automatically find all devices on the network.
discovery.step3Desc: After successful discovery, you can assign radio stations to preset buttons (1–6).
discovery.manualIpsConfigured: Manual IP addresses have been configured. These are used in addition to automatic discovery.
discovery.helpSameNetwork: Check if devices are on the same Wi-Fi as OpenCloudTouch
discovery.helpFirewall: Firewall rules might block device discovery
discovery.helpSetupWizard: Follow the Setup Wizard for step-by-step instructions
